data.title: Applied Optoelectronics secures $71 million order for 800G transceivers
data.description:
• Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) on Thursday said that it has received a new $71 million order for 800G single-mode data center transceivers from a major hyperscale customer.
  • The latest order brings total orders from the customer since mid-March to $124 million and is expected to more than double the company’s existing backlog from that client.
  • The company said it expects to begin deliveries in the second quarter, complete the initial order in the third quarter, and fulfill the new order by year-end.
  • It also recently shipped 10,000 units of 800G transceivers to another hyperscale data center customer.
  • AAOI +2.82% after hours to $106.8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zoom COO Bawa set to resign next month, filing says
data.description:
• Zoom Communications (ZM) Chief Operating Officer Aparna Bawa is set to resign next month, a company filing said on Thursday.
  • Bawa notified the company on March 30, and she will step down effective as of May 8. Her decision was not the result of any disagreement with the company, Zoom added.

data.title: Sony Interactive Entertainment acquires Cinemersive Labs
data.description:
• Sony Interactive Entertainment, a wholly owned subsidiary of Sony Group (SONY), has agreed to acquire Cinemersive Labs, a UK-based machine learning and computer vision company.
  • Following the acquisition, Cinemersive Labs will be integrated into Sony Interactive Entertainment’s Visual Computing Group and tasked with applying machine learning to enhance gameplay visuals and improve rendering techniques for Sony’s PlayStation video games.
  • Terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.

data.title: SpaceX targets more than $2 trillion valuation in IPO - Bloomberg
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) raised its target valuation for an initial public offering to more than $2 trillion. 

Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite, and AI company and its advisers are floating the figure to possible investors in its IPO, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

The new figure eclipses the $1.25 trillion the company was valued at with its purchase of Musk's xAI in February. 

SpaceX didn’t immediately respond to a Bloomberg request for comment

On Wednesday it was reported that SpaceX (SPACE) filed confidentially for an IPO that could happen in June. SpaceX could look for a valuation of more than $1.75 trillion, Bloomberg has previously reported. An IPO for SpaceX would raise as much as $75 billion.

data.title: Wall Street ended mixed as investors digested U.S.-Iran war developments
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages ended mixed on Thursday as markets assessed President Donald Trump’s address to the nation and the latest developments in the Middle East conflict.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished +0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added +0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) dipped -0.1%.

On a sector-by-sector basis, five of the 11 S&P segments concluded in the red, with consumer discretionary at the bottom. At the other end of the spectrum, real estate was the strongest performer. 

Trump said the U.S. will strike Iran “extremely hard” over the next two to three weeks, warning the country would be pushed “back to the stone ages,” though he indicated talks with Tehran remain ongoing. 

"Today was another day where the market tried to assess just how serious the situation in the Strait of Hormuz has become, especially in light of President Trump’s plans to maintain the war for a few more weeks. Interestingly, while we saw weakness, investors seem to be convinced that a way will be found to let ships pass to avoid supply chain breakdowns, especially as the situation in refined products in areas like Southeast Asia is getting dire,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen stated.

Treasury yields were mostly muted across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved down 1 basis point to 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) dropped 1 basis point to 3.79%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.88%. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims  for the week ended March 28 decreased by 9K to 202K, compared to the prior week's revised level of 211K and against the 213K consensus figure.

As for stocks that were on the move, Intel (INTC) climbed +4.8%, while shares of Tesla (TSLA) dropped -5.4%.

data.title: Evogene receives Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• Evogene Ltd. (NASDAQ: EVGN) said on Thursday it had received a notice from the Nasdaq indicating that it had fallen out of compliance with Nasdaq Rule 5550(a)(2) after its shares had closed below $1.00 for 30 consecutive business days.
  • The company said it had until September 28, 2026, to regain compliance, requiring its shares to close at or above $1.00 for at least 10 consecutive business days within the 180-day period.
  • If it had not regained compliance by September 28, 2026, Nasdaq would have notified it that its shares were subject to delisting.
  • The company said maintaining its Nasdaq listing had remained a priority and that it had planned to consider options, including a potential reverse stock split, to regain compliance.
  • Shares -3.16%.

data.title: EACO GAAP EPS of $2.01, revenue of $117.84M
data.description:
• EACO press release (EACO): Q2 GAAP EPS of $2.01.
  • Revenue of $117.84M (+17.7% Y/Y).

data.title: SA Analyst says markets continue to await clarity as Middle East risks cloud growth outlook
data.description:
Wall Street finished largely unchanged on Thursday as investors weighed geopolitical uncertainty against resilient economic signals, with market sentiment hinging on clarity surrounding the Middle East conflict.

“Today was another day where the market tried to assess just how serious the situation in the Strait of Hormuz has become, especially in light of President Trump’s plans to maintain the war for a few more weeks,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen. “Interestingly, while we saw weakness, investors seem to be convinced that a way will be found to let ships pass to avoid supply chain breakdowns, especially as the situation in refined products in areas like Southeast Asia is getting dire.” 

Nelissen added that markets remain in a holding pattern until more concrete policy direction emerges. “Once the market gets a clear signal that goes beyond a social media post from the president, I think we’ll go back to focusing on growth acceleration, fully supported by positive numbers from indicators like the ISM Manufacturing Index.”

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND)  edged up 0.1%, while the S&P 500 (SP500) also gained 0.1%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) slipped 0.1%.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Vericel secures $197M BARDA deal, boosting NexoBrid supply plans
data.description:
• Vericel (VCEL) secured a 10-year contract worth up to $197M from the U.S. BARDA to supply NexoBrid.
  • Additionally, the $35M base contract includes ~$10M over the next 12 months for initial supply, VMI setup, and early development for blast trauma use.
  • The contract, effective April 1, 2026, also allows for optional additional orders to expand the Strategic National Stockpile.

data.title: OTTR, HTO draw highest short interest in utilities sector; BIP and SRE see lowest short interest
data.description:
Within the utilities sector (XLU), short sellers remain heavily concentrated in electric utilities, with several electricity producers and multi-utility companies ranking among the most heavily shorted stocks in the market as of end-March.

Otter Tail (OTTR) and H2O America (HTO) recorded the highest short interest among utilities stocks with a market capitalization of over $2B.

The utilities sector (XLU) comprises companies involved in services like electricity, natural gas, and water, among other services.

Otter Tail (OTTR) was the most shorted stock in the utilities sector, with short interest at 12.92% of its shares outstanding, followed by H2O America (HTO) at 12.30%, reflecting elevated bearish positioning in both stocks.

Otter Tail (OTTR) was rated a Hold by Seeking Alpha Quant ratings. However, Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts are bullish and rated OTTR a Buy.

Seeking Alpha Quant ratings rated HTO as a Sell. However, Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish and have rated it a Strong Buy and Buy, respectively.

At the other end of the spectrum, Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP) and Sempra (SRE) were among the least shorted utilities stocks, with short interest of 0.20% and 1.36%, respectively.

The top five most shorted utilities stocks with a market capitalization above $2B (as a percentage of shares outstanding) are

  • Otter Tail (OTTR) 12.92% short interest
  • H2O America (HTO) 12.30%
  • Oklo (OKLO) 12.18%
  • Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE) 11.89%
  • Black Hills (BKH) 9.26%

The five least shorted utilities stocks, with a market capitalization above $2B, are:

  • PG&E Corporation (PCG) 1.89% short interest
  • Southwest Gas (SWX) 1.77%
  • MGE Energy (MGEE) 1.68%
  • Sempra (SRE) 1.36%
  • Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP) 0.20%
